21bd159d65ab55c6975ff13eb55ff061bc1a2ec7: {"entity_table": "berka_loan", "entity_column": "loan_id", "time_column": "grant_date", "horizon_days": 1825, "val_cutoff": "1997-01-01", "test_cutoff": "1998-01-01", "label_sql": "SELECT loan_id, grant_date AS as_of, CASE WHEN status IN ('B', 'D') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS label FROM berka_loan", "time_columns": {"berka_account": "open_date", "berka_card": "issued", "berka_client": null, "berka_counterparty": null, "berka_disp": null, "berka_district": null, "berka_loan": "grant_date", "berka_order": null, "berka_trans": "trans_date"}, "outcome_columns": ["status"], "framing": {"question": "We're asking, for each loan, whether it will eventually go bad and end in default rather than being paid off cleanly. A default here means the borrower fell into debt or finished the contract without repaying.", "moment": "We make the call the moment the loan is granted, using only what's known that day about the account, the client, their district, and their past transactions. Nothing that happened after the loan was approved is allowed into the prediction.", "honesty": "The loan's status field literally records whether it defaulted, so letting the model see it would be handing over the answer. We hide it and let the model rediscover the risk from the borrower's history instead of copying the outcome."}}
88a3d459c154d8cf7fb28d169a2ad4876a5ed52b: {"entity_table": "berka_loan", "entity_column": "loan_id", "time_column": "grant_date", "horizon_days": 1825, "val_cutoff": "1997-06-01", "test_cutoff": "1998-01-01", "label_sql": "SELECT loan_id, grant_date AS as_of, CASE WHEN status IN ('B', 'D') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS label FROM berka_loan", "time_columns": {"berka_district": null, "berka_client": null, "berka_account": "open_date", "berka_disp": null, "berka_card": "issued", "berka_loan": "grant_date", "berka_order": null, "berka_trans": "trans_date", "berka_counterparty": null}, "outcome_columns": ["status"], "framing": {"question": "We're predicting whether a loan will eventually end badly — that is, fall into default rather than being paid off cleanly. Each loan gets a simple yes-or-no guess about its final fate.", "moment": "We make the call at the moment the loan is granted, using only what the bank knew about the account and customer up to that day. Nothing that happened after the loan was approved is allowed into the prediction.", "honesty": "The loan's status column literally records how things turned out, so letting the model peek at it would be like grading a test with the answer key attached. We hide it (and anything else only knowable later) so the model has to learn from early signals, just as it would for a brand-new loan."}}
